# extensions/ — LLM context file

This directory contains all data source extensions for MyDailyUpdater.
Each extension is a self-contained package: fetch → process → render → FeedSection.

See also: extensions/README.md (human guide), extensions/_template/ (starter package)

---

## File map

```
base.py              BaseExtension ABC + FeedSection dataclass  ← read this first
__init__.py          REGISTRY list — all active extensions registered here
README.md            Human-readable guide (quickstart, checklist, testing)
llms.txt             This file

arxiv/
  __init__.py        ArxivExtension — fetch + LLM score + summarise + figures
  collector.py       fetch_papers(), enrich_papers_with_figures()
  scorer.py          score_papers() — LLM batch scoring, returns filtered list
  summarizer.py      summarize_papers()
  template.md.j2     Jinja2 section template
  head.html.j2       KaTeX CSS/JS injection (renders LaTeX in figure captions)
  nav.md.j2          Per-category quick-nav links
  README.md          Config options, output schema, test commands

hacker_news/
  __init__.py        HackerNewsExtension — fetch + LLM summarise
  collector.py       fetch_stories()
  README.md          Config options, output schema, test commands

github_trending/
  __init__.py        GitHubTrendingExtension — fetch + LLM summarise
  collector.py       fetch_github_trending()
  README.md          Config options, output schema, test commands

weather/
  __init__.py        WeatherExtension — fetch today's forecast, no LLM
  collector.py       fetch_today_weather() — Open-Meteo geocoding + forecast API
  README.md          Config options, output item schema, test commands

postdoc_jobs/
  __init__.py        PostdocJobsExtension — offline by default
  collector.py       fetch_jobs()
  README.md          Config options, enabling steps

supervisor_updates/
  __init__.py        SupervisorExtension — page change detection, offline by default
  README.md          Config options, enabling steps

_template/           Starter package — copy this to build a new extension
  __init__.py        Fully documented extension skeleton with contracts
  collector.py       Fetch logic skeleton (separate module for testability)
  template.md.j2     Jinja2 section template skeleton with usage comments
  README.md          6-section doc template + 10-item PR checklist
```

Note: `postdoc_jobs` and `supervisor_updates` are in `REGISTRY` but **disabled
by default** (`enabled: false` in sources.yaml). Enable by setting `enabled: true`.

---

## BaseExtension contract

```python
class BaseExtension(ABC):
    key:   str = ""    # unique snake_case — must match config/sources.yaml key
    title: str = ""    # display name used in rendered output
    icon:  str = "📌"  # shown in nav + section heading

    def __init__(self, config: dict, llm_client: Any = None): ...

    @property
    def enabled(self) -> bool:
        return self.config.get("enabled", True)

    @abstractmethod
    def fetch(self) -> list[dict]: ...      # no LLM; return raw items

    def process(self, items: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
        return items                         # override for scoring/summarising

    @abstractmethod
    def render(self, items: list[dict]) -> FeedSection: ...  # no network

    def run(self) -> FeedSection:
        # called by orchestrator
        if not self.enabled:
            return self.build_section()
        items = self.fetch()
        items = self.process(items)
        return self.render(items)
```

---

## FeedSection

```python
@dataclass
class FeedSection:
    key:         str             # snake_case identifier
    title:       str             # section heading
    icon:        str = "📌"
    payload_key: str | None = None
    items:       list[dict]      # processed items; schema is extension-specific
    meta:        dict            # stats (counts, durations, etc.)
```

---

## self.config — injected keys

The orchestrator merges `sources.yaml` + `config/extensions/{key}.yaml`
and injects these keys into every extension's config:

| Key | Type | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `enabled` | bool | sources.yaml |
| `language` | str | sources.yaml — BCP-47 code, e.g. "en", "zh" |
| `llm_scoring_model` | str | sources.yaml llm.scoring_model |
| `llm_summarization_model` | str | sources.yaml llm.summarization_model |
| `dry_run` | bool | set when `--dry-run` CLI flag is used |

Extension-specific keys come from `config/extensions/{key}.yaml`.
Example for arxiv:

| Key | Source |
|---|---|
| `categories` | config/extensions/arxiv.yaml |
| `must_include` | config/extensions/arxiv.yaml |
| `boost_keywords` | config/extensions/arxiv.yaml |
| `llm_score_threshold` | config/extensions/arxiv.yaml |
| `max_papers_per_run` | sources.yaml arxiv.max_papers_per_run |

---

## self.llm — LLM client

OpenAI-compatible client (OpenRouter). Standard usage:

```python
resp = self.llm.chat.completions.create(
    model=self.config["llm_summarization_model"],
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
    max_tokens=120,
)
result = resp.choices[0].message.content.strip()
```

Always guard with dry_run:

```python
def process(self, items):
    if self.config.get("dry_run"):
        return items          # skip all LLM calls
    ...
```

---

## Item field conventions

### Papers (produced by arxiv extension)
```
id, title, authors, affiliations, categories, primary_category,
primary_category_anchor, url, score, abstract, keywords_matched,
figure_url, figure_caption
```

### HN stories (produced by hacker_news extension)
```
id, title, url, score, comments_url, summary
```

### GitHub repos (produced by github_trending extension)
```
full_name, url, description, language, stars_today, total_stars, summary
```

### Weather (produced by weather extension)
```
query, label, resolved_name, region, country, latitude, longitude,
timezone, forecast_date, condition, weather_code, temperature_c,
apparent_temperature_c, temp_max_c, temp_min_c, humidity_pct,
wind_speed_kmh, precipitation_probability_pct, is_day, sunrise, sunset,
source, source_url
```

### Jobs (produced by postdoc_jobs extension)
```
title, url, institution, location, deadline, salary, source,
relevance_score, requirements
```

### Supervisor updates (produced by supervisor_updates extension)
```
name, institution, url, change_summary
```

---

## How to add an extension (quick reference)

```bash
# 1. Copy the template package
cp -r extensions/_template extensions/my_source
```

Then in `extensions/my_source/__init__.py`:
- Set `key = "my_source"`, `title`, `icon`
- Implement `fetch()`, optionally `process()`, `render()`
- Put HTTP logic in `collector.py` (keeps it unit-testable)

```python
# 2. Register in extensions/__init__.py
from extensions.my_source import MySourceExtension
REGISTRY = [..., MySourceExtension]
```

```yaml
# 3. Add to config/sources.yaml
display_order:
  - my_source

my_source:
  enabled: true
  max_items: 20  # any source-level limit
```

```yaml
# 4. Optionally add config/extensions/my_source.yaml for filter/keyword config
keywords:
  - AI
llm_score_threshold: 7
```

5. Write `tests/test_my_source.py` — test `collector.py` logic with fixture data.
6. `PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/ -q` — all must pass.
7. Update `extensions/my_source/README.md` with all 6 sections + checklist.

Full guide: extensions/README.md

---

## Testing extensions

```bash
# Run all tests (no network, no LLM)
PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/ -q

# Run tests for one extension
PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/test_arxiv_collector.py -v

# Smoke test with live data, zero LLM cost
python main.py --dry-run
```

Tests target the `collector.py` functions — not the extension class directly.
Use inline fixture data; no live API calls in the test suite.
